title: Cephalocereus luetzelburgii
uses: Cephalocereus luetzelburgii is a cactus species that has been reported to have potential medicinal properties. It is said to contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. taxonomy: Cephalocereus luetzelburgii is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is native to Mexico and is classified as a genus within the subfamily Cactoideae. The subfamily Cactoideae is part of the order Caryophyllales, which includes other flowering plants such as carnations and hollyhocks. The order Caryophyllales is further grouped under the class Magnoliopsida, which is a subclass of flowering plants. This classification places Cephalocereus luetzelburgii within the larger plant kingdom, specifically in the division Magnoliophyta.
